  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "Class IX",
      "subject": "Mathematics",
      "part_or_section": "Curriculum Structure and Question Paper Design",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Number System",
          "marks": 7,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation of rational numbers on the number line",
            "Density of rational numbers and its proof",
            "Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers",
            "Decimal representation of rational numbers",
            "Introduction to irrational numbers",
            "Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3",
            "The square root spiral"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Number System"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Rational Numbers",
            "Irrational Numbers",
            "Density of Rational Numbers",
            "Decimal Representation",
            "Square Root Spiral"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Introduction to rational numbers",
            "Representation on number line",
            "Proof of density",
            "Finding numbers between two rationals",
            "Decimal representation computation",
            "Concept of irrationality",
            "Proofs for √2 and √3",
            "Construction of square root spiral"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Algebra",
          "marks": 20,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Polynomials",
            "Sequences and Progressions",
            "Exploring Algebraic Identities",
            "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Polynomials",
            "Algebraic Expressions",
            "Sequences and Patterns",
            "Arithmetic Progression (AP)",
            "Geometric Progression (GP)",
            "Algebraic Identities",
            "Factorisation",
            "Linear Equations"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Definition of polynomial, degree, terms, coefficients",
            "Modelling linear growth/decay",
            "Slope and y-intercept (y = ax + b)",
            "Graphing linear equations",
            "Explicit vs Recursive rules for sequences",
            "nth term of AP and GP",
            "Fractals and Tower of Hanoi puzzle applications",
            "Visualising identities using geometric models",
            "Factorisation using algebra tiles",
            "Simplifying rational expressions",
            "Graphical method for solving pairs of linear equations",
            "Substitution and elimination methods",
            "Consistency and inconsistency of solutions"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
          "marks": 4,
          "headings": [
            "Brief history of coordinate geometry",
            "The 2-D Cartesian coordinate system",
            "Distance between two points in the 2-D plane",
            "Midpoint of the line-segment"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Coordinate Geometry"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Cartesian System",
            "Distance Formula",
            "Midpoint Formula",
            "Collinearity and Right Angles"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "History of coordinate geometry",
            "Specifying locations using coordinates",
            "Representing floor plans on grids",
            "Computing distance between two points",
            "Determining if three points are collinear",
            "Computing midpoint position",
            "Checking for right-angled triangles"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Geometry",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Triangles – Congruence Theorems",
            "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)",
            "Circles"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry",
            "Lines and Angles",
            "Congruence of Triangles",
            "Properties of Quadrilaterals",
            "Geometry of Circles"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Euclidean Axioms and Postulates",
            "Angles and Parallelism",
            "Triangle Congruence (SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS)",
            "Quadrilateral Properties and Midpoint Theorem",
            "Circle Chords, Angles, and Cyclic Quadrilaterals"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "History of geometry (India, Egypt, Greece)",
            "Baudhayana's Sulbasutras construction",
            "Euclid's 5 postulates",
            "Definitions: point, line, surface",
            "Linear pair theorem and converse",
            "Vertically opposite angles",
            "Corresponding, alternate, interior angles",
            "Proof by contradiction in geometry",
            "Triangle rigidity and stability",
            "Propositions and their converses (SSA invalidity)",
            "Parallelogram properties and proofs",
            "Midpoint theorem and medians concurrency",
            "Central symmetry and tiling of 4-gons",
            "Circle definitions: chord, diameter, radius, arc, segment, sector",
            "Circumcircle construction",
            "Angles subtended by arcs at centre and circumference",
            "Concyclic points conditions"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Mensuration",
          "marks": 14,
          "headings": [
            "Area and Perimeter",
            "Surface Area and Volume"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Perimeter of Shapes",
            "Heron's Formula",
            "Brahmagupta's Formula",
            "Circumference and Arc Length",
            "Area of Sectors and Segments",
            "Surface Area and Volume of Solids"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Perimeter and Circumference",
            "Areas of Plane Figures (Triangles, Rectangles, Parallelograms)",
            "Heron's Formula",
            "Brahmagupta's Formula",
            "Surface Area and Volume of 3D Solids"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Introduction to Pi and its irrationality",
            "Length of an arc",
            "Squaring a rectangle (Baudhayana)",
            "Derivation of area of circle",
            "Area of sector and segment",
            "Heron's formula as special case of Brahmagupta's",
            "Surface areas/volumes: Cuboids, Cubes, Cylinders, Cones, Pyramids, Spheres (including hemispheres)",
            "Computational thinking for 3D shapes"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U6",
          "unit_name": "Statistics and Probability",
          "marks": 10,
          "headings": [
            "Graphical representation of data",
            "Measures of central tendency",
            "Concept of probability and randomness"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Statistics",
            "Introduction to Probability"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Data Representation (Bar Graphs)",
            "Central Tendency (Mean, Median, Mode)",
            "Probability Scale",
            "Empirical and Theoretical Probability"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Collecting and organising data",
            "Weighted average computation",
            "Reading stacked bar graphs",
            "Concept of randomness",
            "The probability scale (likelihood)",
            "Empirical probability from experiments/data",
            "Theoretical probability: sample space, events",
            "Tree diagrams and tables for probability"
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [
    {
      "title": "Continuous and Holistic Assessment",
      "source_text": "Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process, moving beyond summative examinations.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },
    {
      "title": "Portfolio Assessment",
      "source_text": "Portfolio 05 Marks (Internal Assessment)",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    }
  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Lab Practical",
      "source_text": "Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books (05 Marks)",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Geometric Constructions and Models",
      "source_text": "Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras; Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle; Visualising identities using geometrical models; Drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Square Root Spiral Construction",
      "source_text": "Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.",
      "related_unit": "Number System",
      "related_chapter": "Number System"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [
    {
      "title": "Tower of Hanoi Puzzle",
      "source_text": "Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "title": "Fractals Analysis",
      "source_text": "Analyse attributes of fractals using GP. Applications of GP in fractals.",
      "related_unit": "Algebra",
      "related_chapter": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "title": "Floor Plan Representation",
      "source_text": "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.",
      "related_unit": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "title": "Paper Manipulation and Discovery",
      "source_text": "Practice forming logical converses of statements; Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.",
      "related_unit": "Geometry",
      "related_chapter": "4-gons (Quadrilaterals)"
    },
    {
      "title": "Data Analysis Experiments",
      "source_text": "Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question. Perform experiments for empirical probability.",
      "related_unit": "Statistics and Probability",
      "related_chapter": "Introduction to Probability"
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Mathematical Modelling Projects",
      "source_text": "Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions. Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Cultural Motifs Investigation",
      "source_text": "Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra. Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Total Marks: 80 (Theory) + 20 (Internal Assessment)",
      "Unit I Number System: 7",
      "Unit II Algebra: 20",
      "Unit III Coordinate Geometry: 4",
      "Unit IV Geometry: 25",
      "Unit V Mensuration: 14",
      "Unit VI Statistics and Probability: 10",
      "Question Paper Typology:",
      "- Remembering/Understanding: 43 Marks (54%)",
      "- Applying: 19 Marks (24%)",
      "- Analysing/Evaluating/Creating: 18 Marks (22%)",
      "Internal Assessment Breakdown:",
      "- Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment: 10 Marks",
      "- Portfolio: 5 Marks",
      "- Lab Practical: 5 Marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "None explicitly excluded in the provided text."
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.",
      "Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.",
      "Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.",
      "Emphasis on real-life applications, experiential learning, and cross-disciplinary contexts.",
      "Focus on computational thinking: decomposition, algorithmic problem-solving, visualisation.",
      "Prescribed Books:",
      "- Mathematics - Textbook for class IX (NCERT)",
      "- Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)",
      "- Laboratory Manual - Mathematics, secondary stage (NCERT)",
      "- Mathematics exemplar problems for class IX (NCERT)"
    ]
  }
}
